verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted growth factor, but multiple isoforms (VEGF165, VEGF189, VEGF206) bind heparan sulfate proteoglycans and are retained at the cell surface / ECM in a wash-resistant manner; the membrane-bound vs freely diffusible balance is isoform-dependent. Additionally, VEGFA exists as a disulfide-linked homodimer that is captured on producer and target cells via HSPG tethering before proteolytic release. Clinical anti-VEGFA programs (bevacizumab, ranibizumab) target the soluble pool, but the HSPG-anchored cell/matrix-associated pool is documented. No TM domain in any isoform.
predicted_key_uncertainty: HSPG binding is non-covalent and may be reversible enough to fail strict wash-resistance; could alternatively be secreted_only if matrix deposition is treated as ECM rather than surface.
